main_sections.title: Maheshwar
main_sections.prose_summary: Aarav and Ananya are sitting with their Chachi on a pleasant evening when they spot a picture of a fort in a newspaper. Curious, they ask Chachi about it. Chachi, with a twinkle in her eye, tells them it is the majestic Maheshwar Fort in Madhya Pradesh, built by Queen Ahilya Bai of the Holkar family. She describes the fort's overhanging balconies made of special red stones brought from Gujarat and Rajasthan, and explains how the fort sits on a hill overlooking the Narmada River. Chachi speaks admiringly of Queen Ahilya Bai as a warrior queen who ruled with kindness and wisdom, built temples, schools, and wells for her people, and whose 13-feet tall statue still stands in front of the palace. Ananya and Aarav grow increasingly excited, asking question after question about the fort. Chachi also tells them about Maheshwari sarees — the town's other famous gift — woven from cotton and silk in distinctive patterns. She ends by promising that they will all visit Maheshwar together one day to discover its secrets. The chapter blends cultural heritage, local history, and family warmth into an accessible conversation for young readers.

reading.section_name: Let us Read + Let us Think

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Let us Read — Read the dialogue aloud: Aarav, Ananya, and Chachi discuss the Maheshwar Fort as seen in a newspaper picture. (Full dialogue text spans pp. 125–128.)

Let us Think A — Answer the following.
1. Who was Ahilya Bai?
2. Where is Maheshwar Fort located? What makes its location special?
3. What materials were used to build Maheshwar Fort, and where did they come from?
4. What does the statue of Queen Ahilya Bai in front of the palace represent?

question_bank.stem: Where is Maheshwar Fort located? What makes its location special?
What materials were used to build Maheshwar Fort, and where did they come from?
What does the statue of Queen Ahilya Bai in front of the palace represent?

oracy.section_name: Let us Think + Let us Listen + Let us Speak

tasks_verbatim.task_text:
Let us Think B — Think and discuss.
1. Aarav and Ananya get excited about visiting Maheshwar Fort. What do you think makes historical places like this interesting?
2. If you were Aarav or Ananya, what part of the fort would you be most excited to see — the overhanging balconies, the statue, or the temples? Why?

question_bank.stem: Aarav and Ananya get excited about visiting Maheshwar Fort. What do you think makes historical places like this interesting?
If you were Aarav or Ananya, what part of the fort would you be most excited to see — the overhanging balconies, the statue, or the temples? Why?

tasks_verbatim.task_text:
Let us Listen — Listen to your teacher narrating the following passage about Maheshwari sarees, then arrange the following sentences in the order in which they appear in the passage.
• Later, Maheshwari sarees are made with a blend of silk and cotton.
• Today, these sarees are available both in India and other countries.
• The weavers originally used pure silk.
• The sarees are famous for their reversible borders.

question_bank.stem: Arrange the following sentences in the order in which they appear in the passage: (a) Later, Maheshwari sarees are made with a blend of silk and cotton. (b) Today, these sarees are available both in India and other countries. (c) The weavers originally used pure silk. (d) The sarees are famous for their reversible borders.

tasks_verbatim.transcript_text: The Maheshwari sarees were originally made of pure silk, but cotton yarn was later introduced. As they gained popularity the handloom weaving tradition in Maheshwar began. These sarees are famous for their unique reversible borders and pallus, showcasing a blend of cotton and silk threads. They are traditionally made in colours like peacock blue, bright yellow, forest green, and an Indian red dye called Aal. Today, Maheshwari sarees are available in both national and international markets.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Let us Speak A — Find out from the elders of your home and your locality about any historical place that is there in your town or nearby. You may consider more than one place too! Collect information and pictures of that place and present it to the class. Your presentation must include: Name of the place; Name of the monument; Historical people associated with that monument; Special features of that monument; How the monument is connected to the people (emotionally); Any other relevant information.

question_bank.stem: Find out about a historical place in your town or nearby and present it to the class.

word_work.section_name: Let us Learn + Let us Speak

tasks_verbatim.task_text:
Let us Learn A — The words 'smiling', 'grinning', and 'laughing' have similar meanings. Given below are sets of words that have similar meaning along with a word that does not fit the group. Circle the odd one out.
1. sad, unhappy, tearful, thrilled
2. talk, discuss, kind, speak
3. noisy, quiet, silent, calm
4. cotton, wooden, silk, woollen
Find out two such sets of words and write them below.

question_bank.stem: Circle the odd one out: sad, unhappy, tearful, thrilled
Circle the odd one out: talk, discuss, kind, speak
Circle the odd one out: noisy, quiet, silent, calm
Circle the odd one out: cotton, wooden, silk, woollen
Find out two sets of words with similar meaning and write them.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Let us Learn B — Read the word 'design' aloud. The letter 'g' is silent. Now read the following words and underline the words that have a silent 'g' letter: sign, light, goat, night, sing, sugar, king, high, magnet

question_bank.stem: Underline the words that have a silent 'g' letter: sign, light, goat, night, sing, sugar, king, high, magnet

tasks_verbatim.task_text:
Let us Learn C — Match the following.
A: 1. Kindness  2. Bravery  3. Wisdom  4. Majestic  5. Unique  6. Fierce  7. Dedicated
B: a) Being wise  b) Being royal  c) Being special  d) Being devoted  e) Being strong  f) Being kind  g) Being brave

question_bank.stem: Match the following words with their meanings: 1. Kindness 2. Bravery 3. Wisdom 4. Majestic 5. Unique 6. Fierce 7. Dedicated — a) Being wise b) Being royal c) Being special d) Being devoted e) Being strong f) Being kind g) Being brave
question_bank.table:
Word|Meaning
Kindness|Being kind
Bravery|Being brave
Wisdom|Being wise
Majestic|Being royal
Unique|Being special
Fierce|Being strong
Dedicated|Being devoted

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Let us Speak B — Complete the following passage with suitable Articles (a, an and the). [Passage about a monkey on an oak tree finding an old man's bag containing an apple, a pear, an olive and a carrot.]

question_bank.stem: Complete the following passage with suitable Articles (a, an and the): Once upon _____ time, there live _____ monkey on _____ oak tree. One day, _____ monkey saw _____ old man with _____ bag. _____ naughty monkey snatched the bag. Inside _____ bag was _____ apple, _____ pear, _____ olive and _____ carrot. It gave _____ pear to _____ elephant and _____ carrot to _____ bear. It kept _____ olive and _____ apple inside _____ empty box. While _____ monkey slept, _____ dog found _____ apple and stole it. The monkey was left with only _____ olive to eat.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Let us Write A — Imagine you are visiting Maheshwar Fort with your family. Write a short description of what you see and feel as you explore the fort's balconies and temples.

question_bank.stem: Imagine you are visiting Maheshwar Fort with your family. Write a short description of what you see and feel as you explore the fort's balconies and temples.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Let us Write B — Based on the facts that you have learnt in the lesson, write a short paragraph on the Maheshwar Fort.

question_bank.stem: Based on the facts that you have learnt in the lesson, write a short paragraph on the Maheshwar Fort.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Let us Do — I. Draw a template of a puzzle on thick chart paper/cardboard. II. On the back side of the puzzle draw a picture of a fort. III. Cut the puzzle using child-friendly scissors along the lines to separate into puzzle pieces. IV. Mix the puzzle pieces and try rearranging them into a fort. Play with your friends.

question_bank.stem: Make a fort jigsaw puzzle: draw a puzzle template on cardboard, draw a fort on the back, cut into pieces, and rearrange with friends.

visual_stimulus.brief_description: Puzzle template grid and completed fort jigsaw illustration
